description: Meta-RootFile-Manager exposes CLI-first governance for channelized root files and a unified AGENTS maintenance entry backed by a centered push mainline.
must_use_sequence: Call contract before consuming runtime guidance for this skill.
When handling a natural-language AGENTS maintenance request, call agents-maintain first so the system can rank governed targets, classify semantics, and choose Part A or embedded payload placement.
When editing a governed target with an already known external path, target-contract may still be used for explicit inspection.
agents-payload-contract remains available for narrow payload-only surgery, but it is no longer the primary daily AGENTS maintenance entry.
Treat returned JSON payloads as the primary runtime instruction source.
For daily AGENTS maintenance, update the internal AGENTS_human.md truth source first, then centered-push external Part A, then run lint.

Before declaring AGENTS governance clean, remove installed/runtime legacy sidecars, prune repo-tracked orphan AGENTS mappings, and keep installed managed targets synchronized with the repo-tracked mirror.
Use new-writeback to finalize scaffolded AGENTS targets after replace_me fields are concretely filled or set to N/A.

notes: repo-tracked managed mirrors stay under assets/managed_targets/AI_Projects
runtime-local or ephemeral managed mirrors stay under Codex_Skill_Runtime/<skill>/managed_targets
timestamped stage logs stay under Codex_Skill_Runtime/<skill>/logs/<stage>/

owner_rule: every governed target must expose a path-derived descriptive owner string; if managed content is json inject owner into that json, otherwise inject owner through frontmatter in the same managed file
agents_payload_rule: skill name plus minimal description may appear only in default_meta_skill_order; daily AGENTS maintenance must prefer agents-maintain, while embedded payload-only surgery may still use agents-payload-contract
hard_constraints: Do not treat markdown mirrors as the primary runtime instruction source.
Do not bypass agents-maintain for normal AGENTS maintenance; it is the only unified entry that may perform target ranking, placement gating, mutation, centered push, and lint as one chain.
Do not edit AGENTS_human.md embedded payloads ad hoc; use agents-maintain or the dedicated payload contract.
Do not add extra reminders, extra routing, or extra obligations beyond the user request when normalizing payload semantics.
Do not treat collect as part of the normal AGENTS maintenance loop; collect is retained only for exceptional reverse-sync or external recovery cases.
Do not bypass lint after AGENTS maintenance writeback.
Do not leave repo-tracked orphan AGENTS mappings, installed managed-target drift, or runtime legacy AGENTS_machine sidecars in place; lint must reject them.
Do not leave replace_me in an AGENTS target that is being finalized by new-writeback.
Do not repeat parent AGENTS part_a or parent payload semantics inside child AGENTS surfaces; lint must reject parent-child duplicates except skill entries.
ephemeral_runtime_discovery_policy: default scan/lint/collect/push exclude runtime managed targets under ephemeral_workspace unless the source path is explicitly requested
